Predict pedestrians' violating road-cros.claim_text: Instrumental attitude was a significant predictor of pedestrian violating crossing behavior intention across all four models tested
Descriptive norm was a stronger predictor than subjective norm, and when descriptive norm was added to the model, subjective norm became non-significant
Conformity tendency was a significant predictor in Model 4, indicating that pedestrians are more likely to cross against traffic lights when they observe others doing so
Perceived behavioral control was not a significant predictor in any model, likely because crossing the street is not perceived as challenging for able-bodied pedestrians
Perceived risk was not a significant predictor, suggesting pedestrians fail to adequately perceive the susceptibility and seriousness of risks associated with traffic violations

tasks: Predict pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions

claims.claim_text: Instrumental attitude was a significant predictor of pedestrian violating crossing behavior intention across all four models tested
Descriptive norm was a stronger predictor than subjective norm, and when descriptive norm was added to the model, subjective norm became non-significant
Conformity tendency was a significant predictor in Model 4, indicating that pedestrians are more likely to cross against traffic lights when they observe others doing so
Perceived behavioral control was not a significant predictor in any model, likely because crossing the street is not perceived as challenging for able-bodied pedestrians
Perceived risk was not a significant predictor, suggesting pedestrians fail to adequately perceive the susceptibility and seriousness of risks associated with traffic violations
